question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/network/stake to retrieve the latest NetworkStake data including per-node stake distribution, identifying which nodes hold the most stake and are therefore most critical to consensus gossip, enabling targeted disruption of the minimum set of nodes needed to halt transaction propagation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call getLatestNetworkStake() at high frequency to exhaust networkStakeRepository.findLatest() database query resources, causing a DoS on the stake endpoint that prevents legitimate clients from assessing network health and gossip node stake distribution?
